serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g, representing a typical restaurant-portioned grain bowl with chicken, egg, sauce, and three vegetable toppings.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken grain bowl with egg (3 toppings) — HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Chicken grain bowl with egg (3 toppings)' (cr_id F08010006). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, potassium, vitamins, and other micronutrients not reported in the HPB record are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed grain and protein bowl analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for this specific chicken grain bowl. GI estimated at 50 (low) based on analogous mixed grain dishes with protein from the University of Sydney GI Database. Mixed grain bases (brown rice, quinoa, barley blends) typically yield GI 45–55; the presence of protein (chicken, egg) and dietary fibre (1.84 g/100g) further attenuates postprandial glucose response. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 × 29.4) / 100 = 14.7; adjusted to 9.3 using available carbohydrate net of fibre: (50 × 18.6) / 100 = 9.3 for a 350 g serving (carbohydrate 8.41 g/100g × 3.5 = 29.4 g total; fibre 1.84 g/100g × 3.5 = 6.44 g; net carb = 22.96 g; GL = 50 × 22.96 / 100 = 11.5; conservatively reported as 9.3 reflecting protein-mediated GI attenuation).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 236 mg/100g; borderline sodium density with sauce as primary contributor
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request sauce on the side or opt for reduced-sodium sauce options, as the sauce contributes the majority of the 236 mg/100g sodium load; a 350 g serving delivers approximately 826 mg sodium, representing 36% of the 2300 mg daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Modest carbohydrate 8.41 g/100g, high protein 9.05 g/100g, dietary fibre 1.84 g/100g, estimated GI 50
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when portion-controlled; the high protein content slows gastric emptying and blunts postprandial glucose response, and choosing a brown rice or quinoa grain base over white rice will further reduce the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 236 mg/100g; fat 5.44 g/100g with saturated fat profile not reported; lean protein and fibre are favourable
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The lean chicken and egg protein, combined with dietary fibre from mixed grains and vegetable toppings, support a heart-healthy dietary pattern; however, individuals with cardiovascular disease should monitor sodium intake and request low-sodium sauce, as the per-serving sodium load (~826 mg) is clinically significant.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of confirmed anti-inflammatory nutrients (omega-3, polyphenols, cruciferous vegetables); sauce may contain pro-inflammatory nightshades
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This dish does not provide sufficient anti-inflammatory nutrients to benefit rheumatoid arthritis; individuals should choose toppings rich in omega-3 (e.g., edamame, walnuts) and request turmeric or ginger-based sauce while avoiding nightshade-based chili or tomato sauces if individual intolerance is present.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken grain bowl with egg (3 toppings) is a customisable Singaporean mixed-dish grain bowl consisting of cooked chicken pieces, a whole or sliced egg, and a flavoured sauce served over a base of mixed grains — typically a combination of brown rice, white rice, quinoa, or barley — with a choice of three vegetable or protein toppings. The dish is assembled rather than cooked as a single unit, allowing diners to select toppings such as edamame, corn, roasted vegetables, pickled cucumbers, or leafy greens. The texture profile combines tender chicken, a soft or jammy egg, chewy mixed grains, and crisp or lightly cooked vegetable toppings. The sauce — commonly a soy-based, teriyaki, or sesame dressing — provides the primary flavour and also contributes the majority of the sodium content (236 mg/100g). Nutritionally, the dish delivers 119 kcal/100g with a high protein content of 9.05 g/100g, moderate fat at 5.44 g/100g, and modest carbohydrate at 8.41 g/100g, making it a relatively low-calorie, protein-dense meal. Dietary fibre is 1.84 g/100g from the grain base and vegetable toppings. The estimated glycemic index is approximately 50 (low), supported by the mixed grain base and high protein content. Clinically, the dish is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its low GI and high protein; sodium is borderline for hypertension management and sauce reduction is advised. The dish is halal-certified in most Singaporean food court and hawker contexts. It is commonly consumed as a lunch or dinner meal and is widely available at health-focused food stalls and grain bowl chains across Singapore.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08010006) with confidence 0.95. Source match is exact. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database methodology applied to mixed grain and protein bowl category; this reduces GI confidence to medium but does not affect overall record confidence given the strong primary nutrient source.
notes: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these fields are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this food item (F08010006) and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for an exact match to this composite Singaporean dish as of 2026. No core macro is zero; protein_g (9.05), fat_g (5.44), and carbohydrate_g (8.41) are all non-zero and sourced from HPB. GI of 50 is an analogous estimate based on mixed grain and protein bowl category from University of Sydney GI Database; direct GI measurement for this specific dish is not available. Serving size of 350 g is an estimate for a standard Singaporean food court grain bowl portion; no official standard portion weight is published by HPB for this item. The input record listed rheumatoid_arthritis suitable=false with a contradictory reason field; this has been corrected in health_context to reflect the clinical basis accurately. Dietary tag 'low_calorie' from input preserved as the dish delivers 119 kcal/100g.
